short_definition: A structured summary of an MCP surface's tools, mutation posture, schemas, and common workflows.
description: SurfaceCapabilityMap gives agents a first-class, queryable map of what a surface can do: read, write, command, or report feedback; which tools and schemas exist; which payload-ref affordances apply; and which workflows are recommended or missing. It is meant to reduce guessing and tool-search churn.

authority.notes: The registry chapter is the canonical place for first-class surface capability declarations.

counterexamples: a raw tool list with no affordance or mutation posture
open_questions: Should capability maps be generated by each surface or aggregated by a registry service?

confidence.basis: The registry chapter calls for first-class surface capability summaries to reduce tool-search churn.
